answer:
Klaviyo offers three authentication methods depending on the API endpoint being used [1][2].

For server-side requests to /api endpoints, use private key authentication by setting the Authorization header [1][2]:

```
Authorization: Klaviyo-API-Key your-private-api-key
```

Tech partners building integrations are encouraged to use OAuth to obtain access tokens for server-side calls, which provides improved security and rate limits [1][3].

For client-side requests to /client endpoints, use your 6-character public key (company ID) as a query parameter [2][4]:

```
https://a.klaviyo.com/client/endpoint/?company_id=YOUR_PUBLIC_KEY
```

Private keys can be configured with specific access scopes to limit data exposure [1][2].
